claim: redb is a pure-Rust embedded key-value store requiring no external database daemon or sidecar.
evidence_state: report plus current Rust dependency evidence
required_resolution: Confirm exact redb crate version and build features during ADOPT-001.

claim: redb uses memory-mapped I/O and the report says this bypasses the operating-system cache layer.
evidence_state: report-only wording is technically suspect because mmap normally participates in virtual-memory and page-cache behavior
required_resolution: Verify against redb documentation and source; correct cache-layer wording before architecture acceptance.

claim: redb provides zero-copy B-tree access as native Rust types without serialization.
evidence_state: report-only and may overstate zero-copy and BTreeMap terminology
required_resolution: Inspect redb API and on-disk encoding; distinguish B-tree implementation from std::collections::BTreeMap.

claim: redb provides full ACID transactions.
evidence_state: report claim consistent with intended current use but not independently verified in this capture
required_resolution: Verify crate guarantees and current feature/configuration behavior.

claim: redb uses MVCC so concurrent readers do not block a writer.
evidence_state: report-only concurrency claim
required_resolution: Verify exact reader/writer and transaction concurrency semantics from source and tests.

claim: redb is a Rust library compiled into an application, not a compiler.
evidence_state: clear component-boundary statement
required_resolution: Preserve this distinction in the store decision.

claim: musl static compilation can package redb inside a portable binary and avoid Nix-store RPATH coupling.
evidence_state: report-only portability claim; full static closure depends on every dependency and build output
required_resolution: Prove on the actual dependency closure and target, not from redb purity alone.

claim: redb is described as a high-speed geometry engine for vector coordinates.
evidence_state: redb is a KV engine; geometry behavior must be implemented by application code or another vector library
required_resolution: Do not assign vector-search behavior to redb without locating the implementing code.

claim: cosine similarity is the primary mathematical operation associated with redb vector use.
evidence_state: formula is valid but no evidence that redb performs it natively
required_resolution: Assign cosine computation to the actual application or RuVector owner.

claim: zero-copy f32 reads and AVX-512 SIMD enable 384-4096 dimensional calculations at microsecond speed.
evidence_state: report-only; CPU features, alignment, data layout, implementation, and benchmarks are unspecified
required_resolution: Require reproducible benchmark and implementation-path proof before using latency or SIMD claims.

claim: an external embedding model creates semantic vectors while redb stores and retrieves supplied bytes or coordinates.
evidence_state: architecturally plausible separation
required_resolution: Name the embedding and vector-compute owners separately from storage.

claim: redb is single-node and requires application protocols for distributed state.
evidence_state: report claim aligns with embedded-store shape
required_resolution: Capture distribution and replication as application responsibilities.

claim: redb is key-value only, has no SQL, and lacks native secondary indexing.
evidence_state: report claim requires exact API/version verification but establishes a material design constraint
required_resolution: Verify exact indexing facilities and retain SQL/relational work in PostgreSQL.

claim: redb provides deterministic edge-node latency competitive with LMDB.
evidence_state: report-only without workload, hardware, percentile, durability mode, or benchmark
required_resolution: Define workload and measure p50/p95/p99 plus crash and sync costs.

claim: redb can serve as a local high-frequency agent scratchpad for transient thoughts and state changes.
evidence_state: mapped by report and NotebookLM architecture, not yet accepted as exclusive role
required_resolution: Decide data classes, retention, privacy, and promotion path.

claim: redb can cache embeddings keyed by a BLAKE3 hash to avoid duplicate model inference.
evidence_state: specific hash, key schema, model identity, and invalidation behavior are report-only
required_resolution: Define cache key as content plus model/version/config identity and prove invalidation.

claim: redb can act as an application-level WAL during PostgreSQL latency spikes or outages.
evidence_state: requires ordered synchronization, idempotency, backpressure, replay, and split-brain rules
required_resolution: Decide whether this is WAL, outbox, queue, buffer, or durable authority and define failure semantics.

claim: Nushell or another parser transforms files and symbols into structured AST records.
evidence_state: current CodeDB has parsers and structured rows but coverage varies by language
required_resolution: Map native parsers versus line-oriented fallbacks during ingestion decomposition.

claim: nu_plugin streams structured records through MessagePack directly into redb.
evidence_state: Nushell plugin protocol can use MessagePack, but the exact current CodeDB write path has not been proven by this report capture
required_resolution: Trace the current nu_plugin command-to-store call path and protocol encoding.

claim: redb is the intermediate ingestion buffer between parser output and durable PostgreSQL state.
evidence_state: conflicts with current authoritative CodeDB source-blob and envctl migration uses
required_resolution: Resolve per-data-class roles rather than applying one global intermediate-only rule.

claim: envctl pulls redb blocks, embeds them with all-MiniLM-L6-v2, and commits code plus vectors to PostgreSQL.
evidence_state: target flow is represented, but current envctl and CodeDB implementations are partial and separately owned
task_ids: ADOPT-001; ADOPT-002; POSTGRES-004; POSTGRES-005; POSTGRES-006; STORE-001
required_resolution: Trace current code and split existing implementation from missing integration tasks.

claim: redb serves microsecond tasks while PostgreSQL plus RuVector serves millisecond relational and GNN tasks.
evidence_state: useful tiering hypothesis without measured workload or boundary
required_resolution: Benchmark actual operations and decide by semantics plus latency, not latency alone.

claim: RuVector is a native PostgreSQL extension that avoids a separate vector-database sidecar.
evidence_state: supported by current target architecture and envctl component evidence
required_resolution: Verify extension/client version pairing and live runtime before cutover.

claim: envctl projects a selected PostgreSQL state into files using module_path metadata.
evidence_state: CodeDB already materializes blobs and exports envctl rows; full PostgreSQL-branch projection remains unproven
required_resolution: Adopt existing materialization and define the missing selected-branch projection contract.

claim: Nix or Yazelix compiles the materialized directory into the portable runtime.
evidence_state: mapped to the foundation and end-to-end proof
required_resolution: Prove through isolated source build before profile installation.

claim: redb is passive storage whose ranking changes only through external application or model updates.
evidence_state: useful boundary statement but redb itself does not define ranking semantics
required_resolution: Assign learning and ranking behavior to the actual application or AgentDB layer.

claim: AgentDB .rvf provides SONA reinforcement learning that adapts weights and ranking from agent performance.
evidence_state: report-only in this capture
required_resolution: Verify against AgentDB and RVF source before accepting cognitive authority.

claim: AgentDB files carry MicroLoRA adapters and FastGRNN routing for ruvllm-hosted agent personalities.
evidence_state: report-only in this capture
required_resolution: Verify formats, runtime ownership, hot-swap behavior, and scale against source.

claim: redb is proposed as a strategic embedded foundation for portable bare-metal agent systems, but it is one component rather than the whole intelligence or database architecture.
evidence_state: report conclusion normalized to avoid geometry-engine and zero-dependency overclaim
required_resolution: Use as a design input only after the claim-level verification and owner decision.
